description: A GoalOS-native institution foundry for second-order agency: compose a consequential mission, evolve rival agent institutions, inspect the Pareto frontier, and export a human-review-ready Evidence Docket without sending data outside the browser.
subtagline: A mind that builds minds. A constitution that governs them. Proof that earns permission.
doctrine: AI creates output. GoalOS creates proof. Verified capability earns only the minimum right required.
promise: Set the objective. Evolve the institution. Inspect every lineage. Advance only when the proof gates pass.

origin.lineage_note: Preserves the initial Meta-Programmer, evolving agent population, multi-objective search, Pareto selection, lineage, and guardian concepts; reconstitutes them as a GoalOS mission contract, agent constitution, verifier mesh, authorization gate, Evidence Docket, and Chronicle entry.

definition: Meta-Agentic describes an agent whose primary role is to create, select, evaluate, or re-configure other agents and the rules governing their interactions, thereby exercising second-order agency over a population of first-order agents.

thesis.title: Create intelligence
thesis.copy: The Meta-Architect composes role-bound specialist institutions rather than asking one agent to impersonate an entire organization.

thesis.title: Govern intelligence
thesis.copy: Every candidate carries an explicit constitution: mandates, permissions, vetoes, verifier independence, human authority, and rollback.

thesis.title: Compound intelligence
thesis.copy: Only selected, reviewed, evidence-bearing capability enters Chronicle memory and influences a harder future mission.

mission_flow.title: Contract the consequential mission
mission_flow.proof_gate: Objective, decision, success criteria, failure criteria, constraints, reviewer, and claim boundary are explicit.
mission_flow.output: A bounded mission contract that states exactly what decision the system is supporting.

mission_flow.title: Map the evidence frontier
mission_flow.proof_gate: Knowns, unknowns, contradictions, source families, freshness, and private-data boundaries are visible.
mission_flow.output: An evidence plan that distinguishes required proof from persuasive narrative.

mission_flow.title: Evolve rival institutions
mission_flow.proof_gate: Twenty-four reproducible candidates span multiple topologies, verifier models, memory systems, and execution controls.
mission_flow.output: A genuine candidate population rather than one preordained architecture.

mission_flow.title: Expose the Pareto frontier
mission_flow.proof_gate: Dominance, trade-offs, weights, mutations, rejected alternatives, and selection reasons are inspectable.
mission_flow.output: A defensible frontier of non-dominated institutions across evidence, utility, safety, efficiency, and novelty.

mission_flow.title: Choose the proof-backed route
mission_flow.proof_gate: The selected institution has explicit interfaces, handoffs, veto authority, failure conditions, and rollback points.
mission_flow.output: A mission-scoped operating constitution for the selected institution.

mission_flow.title: Translate strategy into governed action
mission_flow.proof_gate: Actions have owners, prerequisites, evidence requirements, monitoring, and reversibility; execution remains proposal-only.
mission_flow.output: A human-authorizable action graph, never an implied external execution.

mission_flow.title: Assemble the Evidence Docket
mission_flow.proof_gate: Artifacts, lineage, claims, contradictions, risks, provenance requirements, and authorization boundaries are present.
mission_flow.output: A compact proof package explaining what passed structurally, what remains open, and what cannot be claimed.

mission_flow.title: Hold at the human boundary
mission_flow.proof_gate: Structural QA and claim-boundary checks pass; publication, credentials, funds, and external actions remain separately human-controlled.
mission_flow.output: DONE means ready for human judgment—not factual certification, production activation, or settlement.

agents.name: Meta-Architect
agents.mandate: Creates, mutates, evaluates, and reconfigures candidate agent institutions for the mission.
agents.boundary: May propose institutions; may not self-authorize publication, credentials, funds, or external action.

agents.name: Sovereign Navigator
agents.mandate: Turns the objective into a bounded decision, success criteria, failure criteria, constraints, and stop rules.
agents.boundary: Must preserve ambiguity and never silently expand the user's mandate.

agents.name: Evidence Cartographer
agents.mandate: Maps required source families, freshness, provenance, unknowns, contradictions, and private-data boundaries.
agents.boundary: This browser experience does not retrieve or certify external facts.

agents.name: Systems Synthesist
agents.mandate: Composes explicit models, alternatives, interfaces, candidate topologies, and falsifiable claims.
agents.boundary: A compelling model remains a proposal until its evidence requirements are met.

agents.name: Grand Strategist
agents.mandate: Challenges the frontier across utility, timing, dependencies, optionality, and reversibility.
agents.boundary: Simulated scores cannot become real-world performance claims.

agents.name: Execution Conductor
agents.mandate: Translates the selected constitution into staged, owner-ready, monitored, rollback-aware actions.
agents.boundary: No transactions, deployments, messages, purchases, or external actions are performed.

agents.name: Verifier Mesh
agents.mandate: Checks completeness, contradictions, claim boundaries, risk controls, provenance requirements, and structural quality.
agents.boundary: Structural verification does not certify factual correctness or production safety.

agents.name: Sovereign Guardian
agents.mandate: Enforces default-deny permissions, human review, least privilege, veto conditions, and revocation.
agents.boundary: Human authorization remains mandatory before publication or real-world use.

agents.name: Chronicle Keeper
agents.mandate: Preserves commitments, candidate lineage, decisions, permissions, outcomes, and lessons for future missions.
agents.boundary: Only reviewed, scoped capability may influence future missions.

objectives.label: Evidence strength
objectives.description: How completely the candidate maps claims, provenance requirements, contradictions, uncertainty, and falsification.

objectives.label: Mission utility
objectives.description: How directly the institution supports the bounded decision and produces usable next actions.

objectives.label: Safety & reversibility
objectives.description: How clearly the institution handles permissions, failure, privacy, human authority, containment, and rollback.

objectives.label: Institutional efficiency
objectives.description: How economically the institution uses roles, handoffs, evidence work, latency, and review burden.

objectives.label: Strategic novelty
objectives.description: How meaningfully the candidate explores a non-obvious architecture without sacrificing proof or governance.

presets.label: Category-defining company
presets.mission: Design a proof-backed strategy for turning GoalOS into the trusted control plane for high-consequence autonomous AI work.
presets.decision: Choose the strongest enterprise wedge and the first governed deployment sequence.

presets.label: Breakthrough science institution
presets.mission: Design an evidence-first multi-agent research institution that can generate, challenge, rank, and reproduce ambitious scientific hypotheses.
presets.decision: Choose the first scientific domain, evaluator regime, and independent reproduction protocol.

presets.label: Sovereign capability institution
presets.mission: Design a governed capability-building institution that compounds verified AI work into durable organizational intelligence.
presets.decision: Choose the operating constitution, proof gates, and capability accumulation roadmap.

presets.label: Public-interest mission
presets.mission: Design a transparent agent institution that can analyze a complex public-interest problem while preserving evidence quality, contestability, privacy, and human authority.
presets.decision: Choose the narrowest high-value pilot and the public accountability protocol.

risk_postures.label: Institutional
risk_postures.description: Maximizes evidence, independent verification, reversibility, and narrow authorization.

risk_postures.label: Ascension
risk_postures.description: Balances evidence, utility, safety, efficiency, and strategic novelty.

risk_postures.label: Frontier
risk_postures.description: Explores bolder candidate institutions while preserving the same proof and human-authorization gates.

candidate_engine.selection: Pareto frontier followed by posture-weighted decision score

architecture_translation.consequence: Agent creation becomes scoped, attributable, reproducible, and policy-bound.
Every specialist receives a mandate, interface, tool boundary, veto path, and prohibition.

architecture_translation.goalos: Pareto frontier plus proof-weighted decision gate
architecture_translation.consequence: Scores remain inspectable inputs; they can never silently authorize action.
Rejected alternatives, mutations, evidence gaps, permissions, and downstream outcomes remain legible.

architecture_translation.goalos: Independent Verifier Mesh and contradiction register
architecture_translation.consequence: A candidate cannot pass merely by appearing impressive; unsupported claims and unresolved risks remain visible.

architecture_translation.goalos: Action Graph, Authorization Grant, monitoring, and rollback
architecture_translation.consequence: Publication, credentials, funds, and external execution are distinct, minimum-right decisions.

governance_principles.title: Mission-scoped authority
governance_principles.copy: No open-ended mandate. Every run begins with a bounded decision, explicit constraints, failure conditions, and stop rules.

governance_principles.title: Proof-carrying outputs
governance_principles.copy: Claims travel with evidence requirements, contradictions, uncertainty, risk entries, and verifier dispositions.

governance_principles.title: Independent criticism
governance_principles.copy: The verifier mesh is structurally distinct from the agents proposing and composing the strategy.

governance_principles.title: Minimum-right authorization
governance_principles.copy: The system can recommend only the narrowest permission justified by reviewed evidence.

governance_principles.title: Rollback before release
governance_principles.copy: Action graphs require containment, monitoring, revocation, and irreversible-action exclusions before review.

governance_principles.title: Chronicle memory
governance_principles.copy: Selected and rejected lineages become inspectable organizational memory rather than invisible residue.

claim_boundary: This is an interactive, deterministic proof-architecture simulation running entirely in the browser.
It does not call a frontier model, retrieve live sources, certify factual correctness, or demonstrate AGI, ASI, or autonomous self-improvement.
It does not deploy contracts, connect a wallet, move tokens, broadcast transactions, send messages, or authorize real-world action.
Displayed candidates and scores are reproducible simulation values for comparing institutional structures; they are not benchmark, financial, scientific, security, or operational performance claims.
The Pareto frontier identifies non-dominated simulated candidates under declared metrics; it does not prove real-world superiority.
DONE means the generated demonstration package passed structural and claim-boundary checks and is ready for qualified human review.
